mapping.a_element: modular form with pole structure governing degeneracies of dyonic states
mapping.b_element: assignment of discrete subgroups of PSL2 to nodes of the affine E8 Dynkin diagram

mapping.a_element: contour prescription resolving ambiguity in counting formula due to poles
mapping.b_element: elementary properties of PSL2 explaining the correspondence between nodes and subgroups

mapping.a_element: walls of marginal stability where bound states (dis)appear
mapping.b_element: conjugacy classes in the Monster or Conway group linked to Dynkin nodes

brief_justification: Paper A relies on the pole structure of Siegel modular forms and contour integration to resolve degeneracies in dyonic state counting, while Paper B uses group-theoretic properties of PSL2 and conjugacy classes to explain a correspondence between Dynkin diagrams and discrete groups. The underlying mechanisms—modular forms with analytic continuations versus group-theoretic assignments—are distinct, despite superficial parallels in addressing ambiguities or correspondences.
